# Reader and Claim Audit

## Reader map

The one-paragraph manuscript clearly presents a static signaling model. A reader would understand the proposition to claim global uniqueness.

## Claim consistency

The global wording conflicts with the equality boundary covered by `LOGIC-01`.

## Is the argument convincing?

The setup is convincing, but the proof-to-claim chain is incomplete at equality. A tie-breaking rule or a bounded proposition resolves it.

## Data-limitation fairness

Not applicable; the fixture is theoretical.

## Tone and writing

The report describes the logical mismatch without attributing motive or competence. No typographical issue was found.
